keyPoints: Data is stored in flexible, JSON-like documents (BSON).
Schemas are dynamic, meaning the structure of documents can vary.
It is highly scalable and designed for high-performance data persistence.
It supports rich query language for retrieving and manipulating data.
It is ideal for content management systems, catalogs, user profiles, and other hierarchical data.
commonMistakes: Thinking there is no schema; while flexible, an application-level schema should be enforced.
Using deeply nested arrays excessively, which can lead to performance issues during queries.
Forgetting to create indexes for frequently queried fields, leading to slow collection scans.
